title: Practice - Undo Last Commit and Delete Changes

content.problemStatement:
**Scenario:**

- You have a file named `task.txt` that was previously saved and committed in Git.
- Later, a wrong commit was made that you don’t want at all. (i.e a new line was added in `task.txt` with text: "Temporary test entry".)
- At this point, your commit history looks like:


---

**Your Task:**

Undo the last commit and delete all changes made in that commit so that `task.txt` is restored exactly to the state it had in the previous commit.

**Expected Output :**
